output.task: Provide a comprehensive diagnostic and management plan for a 1‑year‑old male Sphynx kitten with chronic diarrhea and poor weight gain.

solution_steps.title: Comprehensive history & physical examination
actions: Record detailed diet, environmental exposures, travel history, and contact with other animals.
Perform a thorough abdominal palpation, assess mucous membranes for dehydration, and measure current weight.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Identify obvious risk factors and rule out systemic disease or dehydration.

solution_steps.title: Baseline laboratory workup
actions: Complete blood count (CBC) with differential.
Serum biochemistry panel including albumin, liver enzymes, electrolytes.
Fecal flotation and concentration for parasites.
Fecal PCR panel for coronavirus, Giardia, Coccidia.
Bacterial culture if indicated.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Detect anemia, hypoalbuminemia, electrolyte imbalances, or infectious agents contributing to diarrhea.

solution_steps.title: Nutritional assessment
actions: Evaluate current kitten food for caloric density and ingredient list.
Consider a hydrolyzed or hypoallergenic diet if intolerance suspected.
Assess feeding schedule and total daily caloric intake.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Determine whether dietary factors are limiting growth.

solution_steps.title: Evaluate for exocrine pancreatic insufficiency (EPI) and chronic enteropathy
actions: Fecal fat quantification.
Serum feline pancreatic lipase (fPLI).
Abdominal ultrasound if indicated.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Rule out malabsorption due to EPI or intestinal lymphoma.

solution_steps.title: Empiric therapy initiation
actions: Start metronidazole 10–15 mg/kg PO q12h for bacterial overgrowth.
If inflammation suspected, add prednisolone 0.5–1 mg/kg PO q24h for a short course (e.g., 7 days).
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Reduce bacterial overgrowth/inflammation and improve stool quality.

solution_steps.title: Supportive care & monitoring
actions: Offer oral rehydration solution or subcutaneous fluids if dehydrated.
Supplement with multivitamins/minerals.
Weigh the kitten weekly and record growth curve.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Restore hydration status and track weight gain trajectory.

solution_steps.title: Re‑evaluation after 1–2 weeks
actions: Repeat fecal exams, CBC/biochemistry.
Adjust treatment based on response (e.g., extend antibiotics or change diet).
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Confirm resolution of diarrhea or identify need for advanced diagnostics such as endoscopy.

solution_steps.title: Long‑term management
actions: Once stabilized, transition to a maintenance diet.
Schedule follow‑up visits every 3–6 months and update vaccinations.
Educate owner on monitoring stool quality and weight gain.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Ensure continued growth, prevent relapse, and maintain overall health.

quality_self_assessment.justification: The plan follows evidence‑based guidelines for feline chronic diarrhea, includes comprehensive diagnostics, empiric therapy, supportive care, and clear follow‑up steps.
